global.title: Data Duplication Analysis

coreIdeas.contextPhrase: Top-level map: four distinct storage layers with different durability profiles.
Native reactor already stores every op twice (PGlite + Postgres) — the baseline this analysis starts from.
PGlite as primary, Swarm /bytes as durable backup — the role split.
Swarm plugin mirrors the Switchboard duplication pattern, swapping the remote layer — structural symmetry.
Dual-mode cost: running both Switchboard and Swarm triples op storage to 3x.
Each copy serves a distinct role (local read, multi-device sync, self-sovereign) — why duplication is acceptable.
Collaboration adds SyncEnvelope wrappers / per-user feeds but not raw-op duplication — scope the live-collab cost.
Typical user totals ~120 KB across all layers — a numeric anchor for capacity planning.

global.description: Where Connect's bytes live: the four storage layers, native 2x duplication (PGlite+Postgres), 3x when Switchboard+Swarm run together.
global.orientation: Running Connect over multiple transports has a real storage cost. This MOC consolidates the duplication analysis: operations live in up to four layers (PGlite, Postgres via Switchboard, Swarm /bytes, per-user op feeds), each with distinct durability and read characteristics. The native dual-store already puts every op in PGlite and Postgres; adding Swarm mirrors the same pattern at the remote layer, raising total cost to 3x. Collaboration adds SyncEnvelope wrappers and per-user feeds but does not re-duplicate raw ops. A typical small user totals ~120 KB — the order-of-magnitude baseline for capacity discussions.
